page: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 is a dedicated CPU server instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features an AMD EPYC processor with 48 virtual CPUs, corresponding to 24 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with 188.0 GB of RAM, providing approximately 3.91 GB of memory per core. Local storage is provided by a bundled 960 GB SSD. The server does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the ccx63 demonstrates strong multi-core and single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng tests and PassMark CPU scores. Cryptographic and database operations are highly efficient, with PassMark encryption and database tests scoring in the top 25%, alongside Redis SET operations reaching 64.55 million operations per second. Software compilation with Clang and ray tracing tasks also register in the top 25% tier. Memory write bandwidth for large uncached blocks is strong at 95.71 GB/sec, while read bandwidth and web serving throughput remain in the average tier. For language model workloads, the instance achieves top-tier performance in text generation for small models, though performance scales down to average levels for medium and large models.

Given its dedicated AMD EPYC cores and balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio of nearly 1:4, the ccx63 offers high resource density and predictable performance for demanding workloads. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures that performance remains consistent without noisy-neighbor interference, making it a cost-effective choice for multi-threaded applications. This server is highly suited for database hosting, software compilation pipelines, parallel processing tasks, and small-scale machine learning inference.
description: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 is a dedicated CPU server featuring 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores) powered by an AMD EPYC processor on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 188.0 GB of RAM, a 960 GB local SSD, and no GPU. The instance delivers strong multi-core performance, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng, software compilation, and database operations, with Redis SET operations reaching 64.55 million operations per second. While memory read bandwidth and web serving throughput are average, its dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent execution. This resource profile makes the ccx63 highly suitable for database hosting, CI/CD compilation pipelines, and parallel processing workloads.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 is a dedicated CPU server featuring 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 188 GB RAM, and a 960 GB SSD, delivering strong multi-core performance for databases and compilation workloads.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud ccx63 is a dedicated AMD EPYC server with 48 vCPUs, 188 GB RAM, and 960 GB SSD storage, optimized for database and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC server with forty-eight virtual cores and balanced memory, optimized for consistent multi-threaded compute and database workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores
188.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 960 GB local SSD
Strong multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and database operations
Delivers 64.55 million Redis SET operations per second
Best suited for database hosting, CI/CD pipelines, and parallel processing
